headline: Truist announces second quarter 2026 earnings call details
summary: Truist Financial Corporation (NYSE: TFC) will report second quarter 2026 financial results before the market opens on Friday, July 17, 2026. Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Bill Rogers and Chief Financial Officer Mike Maguire will host a conference call to review the company's financial results at 8 a.m. ET.

headline: 3 Large-Cap Stocks We Keep Off Our Radar
summary: Large-cap stocks are known for their staying power and ability to weather market storms better than smaller competitors. However, their sheer size makes it more challenging to maintain high growth rates as they’ve already captured significant portions of their markets.

raw.headline: 3 Large-Cap Stocks We Keep Off Our Radar
raw.summary: Large-cap stocks are known for their staying power and ability to weather market storms better than smaller competitors. However, their sheer size makes it more challenging to maintain high growth rates as they’ve already captured significant portions of their markets.

headline: CIM Group’s Permanent Power Company Closes Approximately $600M Construction Financing Facility for Grape Solar and Energy Storage Project
summary: LOS ANGELES, June 18, 2026--CIM Group’s Permanent Power Company closed an approx $600 M construction financing facility for solar and energy storage project in CA ("Grape").

headline: Northern Trust (NTRS) Stock Faces Mixed Analyst Target Changes After Earnings Rework
summary: Northern Trust’s latest analyst update keeps the fair value estimate unchanged at US$171.0, even as Street price targets around the stock move in different directions. A cluster of recent target hikes and cuts, tied to refreshed analyst models, highlights how opinions split on where Northern Trust should trade after new research and earnings work their way through forecasts. In the sections that follow, you will see how these shifting targets fit into the broader story and how to track the...

headline: Is the Departure of Fiserv's CEO a Major Red Flag?
summary: Fiserv recently said that its CEO, Mike Lyons, had resigned to pursue another opportunity.
